title: First Law of Thermodynamics
content: Energy cannot be created or destroyed; it can only be converted from one form to another.  For a closed system: ΔU = Q − W.
extended_content: The internal energy change (ΔU) of a system equals the heat added (Q) minus the work done by the system (W).  In differential form: dU = δQ − δW.  For a flow process the steady-flow energy equation adds enthalpy, kinetic energy, and potential energy terms.
source: Cengel & Boles, Thermodynamics: An Engineering Approach, 8th ed.
